results.taskDescription: Fetch top 15 HN posts and write a paragraph-style summary categorizing them by topic. Tests substantive synthesis from observation data.
results.output:
# Today on Hacker News

The top stories on Hacker News today reveal a fascinating blend of cutting-edge artificial intelligence, deep dives into software engineering, and compelling narratives spanning science and human achievement. From the theoretical limits of machine memory to the practical challenges of building custom hardware, the community is deeply engaged with the future of technology.

### Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning
The conversation around AI remains highly active, focusing not just on capability, but on the ethical and structural implications of the technology. Several top posts address the evolution of AI, including the piece arguing that "AI should elevate your thinking, not replace it." Technical discussions are also robust, highlighted by the Show HN post detailing "AI memory with biological decay (52% recall)," suggesting novel approaches to data retention. Furthermore, the industry is grappling with evaluation standards, as seen in the discussion about why "SWE-bench Verified no longer measures frontier coding capabilities."

### Software Engineering & Cybersecurity
The technical depth of the top posts is evident in the security and programming categories. Cybersecurity remains a critical topic, with a detailed look at "Fast16: High-precision software sabotage 5 years before Stuxnet," offering historical context to modern threats. On the development front, there is significant interest in low-level programming, ranging from tutorials like the "Clay PCB Tutorial" to advanced discussions on embedded systems, such as "Running Bare-Metal Rust Alongside ESP-IDF on the ESP32-S3's Second Core." The community also explored foundational programming concepts, including memory management techniques like "Box to Save Memory in Rust."

### Science, Environment, and Physical Achievement
Beyond the digital realm
